description:
src/lib.rs:311: 'crc_str.parse::<i64>().map(|n| n as u32)'. Clippy flags this as cast_possible_truncation + cast_sign_loss.

Today this is observably benign because GCP serializes uint32 CRC32C through proto3 int64 -> JSON string, so values are always in [0, 2^32-1] and the cast is exact. But:
  - parse::<i64> accepts negative numbers, '-1' -> i64=-1 -> u32=0xFFFFFFFF (wraparound). A response with a negative dataCrc32c would silently produce a wrong expected value, then the CRC check would fail loudly with 'CRC32C integrity check failed' rather than 'invalid dataCrc32c value'. Failure mode is correct but the error message misleads.
  - parse::<i64> accepts values up to i64::MAX, casting to u32 truncates the high bits silently. Same observation: the resulting CRC will mismatch and the failure surfaces as DecodeFailed with the wrong reason.

Suggested fix:
  let expected = crc_str.parse::<u32>().map_err(|e| SecretError::DecodeFailed(format!("gcp-sm: invalid dataCrc32c value (must be 0..=u32::MAX): {e}")))?;

This is one line, removes both clippy lints, and makes the contract explicit.
title: i64 -> u32 cast in CRC32C parse silently truncates / sign-loses
